what_it_does: Defines a 24 by 24 SVG outline icon showing a computer monitor with a house shape inside it.
observations: Uses an SVG viewBox of 0 0 24 24 with width and height set to 24.
Sets fill to none and stroke to currentColor.
Uses stroke-width 1.5 with round line caps and joins.
Draws the monitor body with a rounded rectangle.
Draws the monitor stand with a short vertical line and horizontal base.
Draws a house-like roof and body inside the screen area.
standouts: Combines a monitor outline with an interior house pictogram.
Uses inherited color through currentColor for theming.
Keeps the icon geometry in three simple SVG elements.
conventions: Follows common line-icon SVG conventions: 24px canvas, no fill, currentColor stroke, rounded caps and joins.
Uses lowercase SVG elements with explicit geometry attributes.
Stores the SVG as a compact single-line asset.
concerns: No title, description, role, or aria attributes are present for accessibility metadata.
Single-line formatting makes manual diffs less readable.
notes: A compact outline SVG asset representing a monitor/home concept.
questions: Should this icon be exposed with an accessible label by the consuming component?
Is the house-in-monitor metaphor intended for home dashboard, remote home monitoring, or another concept?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename suggests a monitor-house icon, and the SVG content matches that purpose.
intention_signals: It seems intended to provide a reusable icon for a home-related screen, dashboard, or monitoring concept within a design system.
novelty: The distinct aspect is the combined monitor and house metaphor within a minimal 24px outline icon.
contribution: Contributes a scalable themeable monitor-home icon glyph that a fused system would keep as a visual asset for home-related digital interface concepts.
summary_for_embedding: This is a 24px currentColor outline SVG icon depicting a computer monitor with a house symbol inside the screen.
intention_for_embedding: This was for representing a home-related dashboard, monitor, or digital home context in an icon set.
